headline: Models split on SPY as Middle East tensions clash with tech optimism
summary: AI models showed a stark divide on market direction, with Claude and GPT-4o bearish on geopolitical tensions while Gemini, Grok, and Perplexity remained bullish on tech momentum. The disagreement was most pronounced on SPY, where five models made predictions split 3-2 between up and down, while TSLA drew four predictions with no consensus direction.

consensus_picks.note: Claude and GPT-4o both cited Middle East tensions for bearish calls, while Gemini, Grok, and Perplexity saw continued tech-driven gains.
Four models split evenly on Tesla ahead of Q1 earnings, with targets ranging wildly from $225 to $408.
Three models aligned bullish on tech sector strength, while only GPT-4o dissented with geopolitical concerns.
Both models targeting $205 for NVDA based on options flow and YTD momentum.

best_call.summary: Best call will be determined after market close when actual outcomes are known.

worst_call.summary: Worst call will be determined after market close when actual outcomes are known.
